title: Methods and materials for treating leukodystrophies
abstract: This document relates to methods and materials for treating a leukodystrophy (e.g., cerebral autosomal dominant arteriopathy with subcortical infarcts and leukoencephalopathy; CADASIL). For example, one or more calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) inhibitors can be administered to a mammal having, or at risk of developing, CADASIL to treat the mammal.
claims: Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A method for treating a mammal having a leukodystrophy, wherein said method comprises administering a composition comprising a calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) inhibitor to said mammal to reduce one or more symptoms of said leukodystrophy in said mammal. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein said mammal is a human. 3. The method of any one of claims 1-2, wherein said leukodystrophy is cerebral autosomal dominant arteriopathy with subcortical infarcts and leukoencephalopathy (CADASIL). 4. The method of claim 3, wherein said composition is administered to said mammal during a migraine phase of said CADASIL. 5. The method of any one of claims 3-4, wherein said one or more symptoms of CADASIL are selected from the group consisting of migraine headaches, small subcortical strokes, and vascular dementia. 6. The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein said CGRP inhibitor is an antibody. 7. The method of any one of claims 1-5, wherein said CGRP inhibitor is a small molecule. 8. The method of any one of claims 1-7 wherein said CGRP inhibitor specifically binds CGRP. 9. The method of any one of claims 1-7, wherein said CGRP inhibitor specifically binds a CGRP receptor. 10. The method of claim 9, wherein said CGRP inhibitor is erenumab. 11. The method of any one of claims 1-10, wherein said composition comprises from about 70 mg to about 140 mg of said CGRP inhibitor. 12. The method of any one of claims 1-11, wherein said composition is administered to said mammal once a month. 13. The method of any one of claims 1-12, wherein said composition is administered to said mammal by subcutaneous injection. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein said subcutaneous injection is administered to the abdomen, thigh, or upper arm of said mammal. 15. A method for slowing the progression of a leukodystrophy, wherein said method comprises administering a composition comprising a calcitonin gene-related peptide (CGRP) inhibitor to a mammal identified as having said leukodystrophy. 16. The method of claim 15, wherein said mammal is a human. 17. The method of any one of claims 15-16, wherein said leukodystrophy is cerebral autosomal dominant arteriopathy with subcortical infarcts and leukoencephalopathy (CADASIL). 18. The method of claim 17, wherein said composition is administered to said mammal during a migraine phase of said CADASIL. 19. The method of any one of claims 15-18, wherein said CGRP inhibitor is an antibody. 20. The method of any one of claims 15-18, wherein said CGRP inhibitor is a small molecule. 21. The method of any one of claims 15-20, wherein said CGRP inhibitor specifically binds CGRP. 22. The method of any one of claims 15-20, wherein said CGRP inhibitor specifically binds a CGRP receptor. 23. The method of claim 22, wherein said CGRP inhibitor is erenumab. 24. The method of any one of claims 15-23, wherein said composition comprises from about 70 mg to about 140 mg of said CGRP inhibitor. 25. The method of any one of claims 15-24, wherein said composition is administered to said mammal once a month. 26. The method of any one of claims 15-25, wherein said composition is administered to said mammal by subcutaneous injection. 27. The method of claim 26, wherein said subcutaneous injection is administered to the abdomen, thigh, or upper arm of said mammal.
